title: FEMS V1 feedback integration end-to-end test (bitemporal + outcome + pinned + hygiene + degradation + calibration + replay + routing + trace + spawn distill)
goal: End-to-end integration test covering the full D.3 FEMS V1 feedback integration chain: build a capsule under RetrievalModeRouter, inject + record + attach outcome, run hygiene + check calibration + run replay + export trace + harvest spawn-tree candidates. Validates that all 10 folded WP-1-FEMS-* stub primitives compose against the shared MemoryCapsule + FEMS V1 surfaces.
expected_diff_shape: New integration test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/fems_integration_e2e_tests.rs. Reuses fixtures from D.1 (sample_fems_items.json) + adds tests/fixtures/fems_integration_e2e/spawn_tree.json for the spawn-tree harvest path.
implementation_notes: Test flow: (1) build a capsule with RetrievalModeRouter (assert Hybrid mode for general query); (2) build same capsule with exact uuid query (assert NoRag mode); (3) build same capsule with hbr_id_pattern query (assert AuthoritativeOnly mode); (4) inject + record capsule via D.1 path; (5) attach Pass outcome via MT-158, assert per-item scores updated; (6) pin one item via MT-159, assert outcome feedback skips it on next attach; (7) run hygiene job via MT-160, assert one consolidation candidate emitted and pinned items skipped; (8) generate retrieval under simulated load_signal=0.9, assert DegradationReport shows graph + re-rank skipped; (9) read calibration snapshot via MT-162, assert degradation_rate signal updated; (10) run replay via MT-163 against the original capsule, assert replay_succeeded with empty ReplayDifference; (11) export trace via MT-165 with default redaction policy, assert pii redacted in bundle; (12) opt-in a fixture session via DISTILL_CORPUS=true and harvest spawn tree via MT-166, assert candidate emitted only for Pass-outcome parent-child pair; (13) assert bitemporal indexing via MT-157 returns the right items at AsOfQuery snapshots both before and after hygiene invalidation. Run with cargo test --release; logged warning if wall-clock >60s.
risk_if_missed: Without end-to-end FEMS feedback coverage the 10 folded stubs may individually pass unit tests but fail to compose; the integration validator cannot verify AC-DISTILL-CONTENT-REVIEW and AC-DISTILL-EDITABLE-SURFACE in concert.
validator_focus: Test exercises every D.3 surface in one run; uses real MT-141 capsules + real RetrievalMode routing; perf logged.

builder_handoff_correction_20260525.operator_instruction: Operator clarified that completed/pass-like states were not validator-reviewed and instructed Kernel Builder to mark everything ready for validator handoff except the blocked item.
builder_handoff_correction_20260525.current_interpretation: Kernel Builder implementation handoff readiness only; no WP/Integration Validator pass or validator-reviewed completion is claimed.

lifecycle.ready_for_validation_reason: Kernel Builder believes implementation/supporting evidence is ready for Integration Validator handoff; validator review remains pending.

minimum_controls: Test covers all four AC-DISTILL-* IDs in one run; partial coverage is a test failure.
Pinned item skip and content-reviewer invocation asserted at the integration level, not just unit level.
Replay correctness asserted via empty ReplayDifference on round-trip.

implementation_evidence.summary: MT-168 added the FEMS V1 feedback integration end-to-end test. The test composes the real D.3 trait/API surfaces across retrieval-mode routing, capsule build/record, outcome feedback, pin IPC, hygiene, progressive retrieval degradation, calibration, replay, trace export redaction, spawn-tree distillation, and bitemporal before/after invalidation checks.

sources_checked: Temporal Workflow Definition determinism and replay constraints: https://docs.temporal.io/workflow-definition#deterministic-constraints
LangSmith dataset versioning and trace-to-dataset evaluation docs: https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/manage-datasets
OpenTelemetry GenAI semantic conventions for observable AI traces: https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/gen-ai/
Hugging Face dataset cards / dataset governance reference: https://huggingface.co/docs/hub/datasets-cards
relevant_patterns_found: Replay/evaluation flows should pin inputs to explicit as-of state and make drift inspectable.
Trace-derived training examples need content review, provenance, stable identifiers, and a review gate before promotion.
Trace bundles should redact PII/credentials by default while preserving structured route, scoring, and degradation context.
reuse_opportunities: Reused the existing FEMS V1 D.3 module surfaces instead of creating product code: BitemporalIndex, OutcomeFeedbackLoop, PinIpcService, HygieneJobRunner, ProgressiveRetriever, CalibrationCollector, ReplayEvaluator, RetrievalModeRouter, RetrievalTraceExporter, and SpawnTreeHarvester.
Reused the existing fems_integration_e2e spawn_tree.json fixture for the opt-in distillation path.
Reused CapsuleRecorder / KernelActionSubmitter to exercise the D.1 capsule record action path.
rejected_options: Rejected adding product code for MT-168 because the contract is an integration test over landed primitives.
Rejected using the sample_fems_items.json fixture because its item_ids are non-UUID strings and MT-163 replay diff vectors require UUID audit ids.
Rejected asserting only isolated module tests because MT-168 is specifically about composition across all D.3 surfaces.
research_basis.selected_approach: Add a single deterministic integration test binary that builds UUID-backed RetrievedItem fixtures, records a capsule through CapsuleRecorder, applies outcome/pin/hygiene/calibration/replay/trace/distillation paths, and asserts every contract step in one run.
risks: Hygiene invalidation is represented by applying the emitted prune candidate to the in-memory bitemporal index inside the test rather than through a persistent write-box adapter.
The test remains in-memory by design and does not exercise a live Postgres bitemporal adapter.
The DISTILL_CORPUS opt-in check uses a process environment variable; Rust test parallelism would matter if more env-mutating tests are added to this binary.
mitigations: The test asserts the review-gated prune candidate before applying the bitemporal invalidation, keeping the review boundary explicit.
Replay is checked against the same BitemporalIndex adapter used by MT-157/MT-163, preserving deterministic as-of semantics without requiring infrastructure credentials.
The current binary has one test and uses an EnvVarGuard to restore DISTILL_CORPUS after execution.
validation_plan: Run the dedicated fems_integration_e2e_tests integration binary.
Run the existing fems_v1_integration_tests regression suite.
Run rustfmt --check, git diff --check, and forbidden SQLite/model-runtime scans on touched files.

red.note: Initial red run failed with Cargo no test target named fems_integration_e2e_tests, proving the E2E target was absent before implementation.

high_roi_additions: One composed regression test verifies all D.3 surfaces interact correctly rather than only passing in isolation.
UUID-backed fixture items keep replay compatible with MT-163 diff vectors and avoid false failures from older non-UUID fixtures.
The test records review-gated boundaries: pin FR event, capsule record action id, hygiene prune/consolidation candidates, trace redactions, and distillation candidate-created event.
Existing fems_v1_integration_tests stayed green, reducing risk of breaking the focused module smoke suite.

proof.note: 13 passed; existing D.3 integration smoke suite remained green.
Formatting check passed after mechanical rustfmt.
Whitespace diff check passed; only Git LF->CRLF warning for Cargo.toml.
No matches; exit 1 is ripgrep no-match.

residual_risks: No Integration Validator has reviewed MT-168; READY_FOR_VALIDATION is implementer handoff only.
A live Postgres bitemporal/hygiene write-box adapter path remains outside this test-only microtask proof.
The initial repomem pre-task receipt #2251 was mislabeled as self-improvement guardrail work; receipt #2252 corrected the active MT-168 scope before implementation.

integration_validator_review.verdict_reason: fems_integration_e2e_tests.rs composes all 10 D.3 surfaces (RetrievalModeRouter -> CapsuleBuilder -> CapsuleRecorder -> OutcomeFeedbackLoop -> PinIpcService -> HygieneJobRunner -> ProgressiveRetriever -> CalibrationCollector -> ReplayEvaluator -> RetrievalTraceExporter -> SpawnTreeHarvester) in one test binary; 1 integration test + 13 fems_v1_integration regression remain green per builder evidence. Uses real production trait surfaces with UUID-backed fixture items so replay diff vectors are compatible with MT-163. Hygiene invalidation is applied to in-memory bitemporal index inside the test (real Postgres adapter not exercised). Single composed test exercises every contract step in one run; assertions include pinned-item skip, content-reviewer invocation, replay round-trip, PII redactions, distillation candidate-created event. CONCERN: only ONE composed test in the binary — single test means one failure trashes the whole composition signal; multiple smaller composed tests (e.g., one per AC-DISTILL-*) would isolate seam failures better.
